text.fullText: Gaá Čhíng Ziíbi Daáwaá Aníšhinaábek Little River Band of Ottawa Indians Natural Resources Department 2608 Government Center Dr. Manistee, MI 49660 Telephone: (231)723-1594 Fax: (231)723-8873 February 17, 2026 Submitted via: https://www.regulations.gov/ U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Office of Water 1200 Pennsylvania Avenue NW Washington, DC 20460 Docket ID No. EPA-HQ-OW-2025-2929: Comments on Clean Water Act section 401 proposed revisions. Dear Administrator and Members of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, I am writing on behalf of the Little River Band of Ottawa Indians (LRBOI) to comment on the proposed revisions to the Clean Water Act Section 401 Water Quality Certification Regulations, published in the Federal Register on January 15, 2026. LRBOI strongly opposes these changes, which threaten our rights and responsibilities to protect our waters. As a federally recognized Tribe with deep ties to our territories in Michigan, the health of our water resources is essential to our culture and well-being. Our treaty rights to hunt, fish, and gather are directly linked to water quality within our ceded lands, making any threat to these waters a threat to our way of life. Section 401 has been a vital tool for Tribes and states to ensure that projects comply with water quality standards. The proposed revisions would narrow certification definitions, impose rigid procedures, and remove necessary flexibility, undermining our ability to protect our resources and creating uncertainty in the certification process. We believe the 2023 Clean Water Act Section 401 Improvement Rule provided a sound framework for consultation and public participation, respecting Tribal sovereignty. We urge the EPA to uphold these principles and reconsider or withdraw the proposed changes completely. LRBOI calls on the EPA to listen to the voices of the Tribes as we request more meetings with Tribal leaders for genuine consultation. The recent public hearing did not feel sufficient. In summary, the LRBOI opposes the EPA's proposed revisions. Our Treaty and Reserved Rights, which are essential for protecting our waters, depend on the effective implementation of Section 401. We are committed to safeguarding our natural resources and urge collaboration to protect our waters, lands, and wildlife. Thank you for your attention to this important matter. LRBOI welcomes any questions or further discussion. Respectfully, Alycia Peterson Great Lakes Policy Specialist Little River Band of Ottawa Indians - Natural Resources Department
